title: Dedicated tenant batching-tier override not taking effect (route policy appears to win over tenant override)
preview:
Issue summary
- Dedicated customer reports that their tenant-level override for batching SLO tier (fast) is not being applied; effective behavior remains standard.
- Customer expectation (and internal design): customer override > route policy > tier default.
- Observed: route policy appears to override tenant settin...
text:
Issue summary
- Dedicated customer reports that their tenant-level override for batching SLO tier (fast) is not being applied; effective behavior remains standard.
- Customer expectation (and internal design): customer override > route policy > tier default.
- Observed: route policy appears to override tenant setting (or tenant setting is ignored).

Customer impact
- Customer is attempting to force fast tier on a latency-sensitive streaming endpoint.
- They are seeing increased queueing and higher p99 compared to pre-tiered-defaults behavior.
- Impact limited to a subset of routes on their Dedicated cluster, but blocks their rollout of tiered defaults.

Environment
- Dedicated cluster: northlake-dedicated-03
- Region: us-east
- Runtime train: 1.22.0 (feature flag enabled for tiered defaults)
- Control plane: 2026.04.08 build (policy evaluation service)

What the customer configured
1)...
links: Customer screenshots (Console override + route policy) attached in Jira (redacted).
Staging repro trace: decision_path shows route policy applied after tenant override (pre-fix).

title: p95 latency regression in eu-west after enabling tiered batching defaults canary

Issue summary:

Customer impact:

text:
Issue summary:
- Customer reports increased p95 latency for streaming chat completions in eu-west that started during Redwood canary rollout of tiered batching defaults (runtime 1.22 train).

Customer impact:
- Northlake reports p95 latency increased from ~1.3s to ~2.4s (their app-level measurement: “time to first token”) on their primary streaming endpoint.
- Impact is isolated to eu-west. us-east appears normal per customer.
- Customer states they did not change client settings; regression correlates with our canary enablement window.

Customer environment:
- Product: Hosted API
- Endpoint: /v1/chat/completions (streaming=true)
- Model: rw-llama-70b-instruct
- Region: eu-west
- Tier: customer did not explicitly set batching tier; traffic subject to default policy during canary.

What changed / suspected trigger:
- Tiered batching defaults canary enabled for a small subset of Hosted ...

title: Oncall users missing access to new Grafana dashboards for rollout auto-rollback (SLO integration)
preview:
Internal oncall responders (SRE + service owners) are receiving links to the new rollout auto-rollback dashboards from incident-bot and the rollout controller audit timeline, but some users see "You do not have permission to view this dashboard" in Grafana.

This blocks oncall triage for the Auto-rollback on SLO bre...
text:
Internal oncall responders (SRE + service owners) are receiving links to the new rollout auto-rollback dashboards from incident-bot and the rollout controller audit timeline, but some users see "You do not have permission to view this dashboard" in Grafana.

This blocks oncall triage for the Auto-rollback on SLO breach integration rollout (especially during game day / staging-to-prod expansion). Request: update access groups/permissions so oncall rotations can view the dashboards by default, and document the process so future dashboard additions don’t repeat this.

Dashboards impacted (from observability-pack PR-913):
- "Rollouts: Auto-rollback events"
- "Rollouts: SLO evaluation outcomes"
- "Rollouts: Monitor staleness / missing data"

Who is impacted:
- #eng-oncall and #eng-sre responders
- Service owners paged on rollback events (non-SRE) who are in standard oncall access groups

I...

title: p95 latency regression in eu-west after enabling FP8 kernel (hosted) — rollback executed
preview:
Customer (NordicBank) reported a latency regression in eu-west beginning ~30–45 minutes after FP8 kernels were enabled for a subset of hosted traffic.

Issue summary
- Symptom: increased p95 end-to-end latency, most visible on chat completions; customer also reported increased time-to-first-token (TTFT) on streaming...
text:
Customer (NordicBank) reported a latency regression in eu-west beginning ~30–45 minutes after FP8 kernels were enabled for a subset of hosted traffic.

Issue summary
- Symptom: increased p95 end-to-end latency, most visible on chat completions; customer also reported increased time-to-first-token (TTFT) on streaming.
- Correlation: aligned with regional rollout flag enabling FP8 attention kernel path.
- Impact: customer observed p95 latency increase of ~18–30% (varies by route/model), intermittent timeouts on longer prompts (rare).

Customer report (from Support intake)
- Time window: 2026-02-23 10:50–12:10 UTC (first noticed), persisted until rollback.
- Affected endpoints: /v1/chat/completions (streaming=true).
- Example request IDs (customer-provided): euw-req-7f2c9e1a, euw-req-80c41b7d, euw-req-91db7c20.

Initial impact assessment (Redwood)
- Region: eu-west only.
- Suspected coho...

title: Latency spike on internal Cost Model API endpoint causing slow Console cost breakdown loads
preview:
Issue summary:

- Root symptom appears to be elevated p95 latency on internal Cost Model API (estimate/breakdown endpoints), which Console backend calls on-demand.

Customer impact:
- Northbridge Security Labs (enterpr...
text:
Issue summary:
- Customer reports Console cost breakdown pages intermittently taking 20–60s to load or timing out.
- Root symptom appears to be elevated p95 latency on internal Cost Model API (estimate/breakdown endpoints), which Console backend calls on-demand.

Customer impact:
- Northbridge Security Labs (enterprise) cannot reliably load Console cost breakdown for the last 24h; internal finance review blocked.
- Console UI shows partial data or "Something went wrong" after ~30s.

Scope / suspected:
- Internal Cost Model API p95 latency spike and occasional 504s between Console backend and cost-model-service.
- Possible regression due to higher request volume and cache-miss heavy query patterns (high-cardinality breakdown dimensions).

Notes:
- This is not a customer calling the Cost Model API directly; it is Console calling internal endpoints on the hot path.
- Console is behind a ...
links: Grafana: cost-model API latency panel screenshot (internal link)
Console backend log excerpt showing timeout + retry amplification (internal link)

title: Perf canary alerting false positive on small sample (FP8/INT8 rollout) — add min-sample + improve metric tagging/aggregation
preview:
Issue summary:
Perf canary fired TTFT/TPOT regression alerts for FP8/INT8 canary cohorts that were later determined to be false positives caused by small sample sizes and aggregation gaps (missing/partial dimensions caused baseline + canary to mix in queries).

Impact:
- Triggered an automated rollback in one region...
text:
Issue summary:
Perf canary fired TTFT/TPOT regression alerts for FP8/INT8 canary cohorts that were later determined to be false positives caused by small sample sizes and aggregation gaps (missing/partial dimensions caused baseline + canary to mix in queries).

Impact:
- Triggered an automated rollback in one region cohort and created follow-up noise in #eng-sre / #incidents.
- Slowed down kernel rollout decision-making (teams did extra triage to prove it was noise).
- Risk: could mask real regressions if alert fatigue sets in.

Context:
Ongoing FP8/INT8 kernel benchmarking + staged rollout. Canary cohorts are low-volume by design, but current alert rules assume a stable sample size and consistent metric tagging.

Current behavior observed:
- Alert triggered on p95 TTFT when canary cohort had <10 requests in the lookback window.
- A single cold-start / cache-miss request dominated the...

title: Dedicated H100 pool throughput drop during FP8/INT8 benchmark window (capacity contention)
preview:
Issue summary:
- Customer on Dedicated (Summit AI) reported a sudden throughput drop (tokens/sec) and elevated queueing during a scheduled window.
- We correlated the drop with internal FP8/INT8 benchmark jobs that inadvertently consumed capacity in the same H100 node pool used for the customer’s Dedicated deploymen...
text:
Issue summary:
- Customer on Dedicated (Summit AI) reported a sudden throughput drop (tokens/sec) and elevated queueing during a scheduled window.
- We correlated the drop with internal FP8/INT8 benchmark jobs that inadvertently consumed capacity in the same H100 node pool used for the customer’s Dedicated deployment.

Customer impact:
- Time window: 2026-02-16 02:10–03:05 UTC
- Symptoms: lower sustained throughput, increased p95 latency, intermittent request timeouts at peak concurrency.
- Scope: Summit AI Dedicated H100 pool in us-east.

Environment:
- Dedicated cluster/pool: dedicated-prod-use1-h100
- Region: us-east

Detection:
- Customer email to Support + Console screenshots showing throughput reduction.
- SRE saw increased scheduler queue depth for the dedicated namespace and elevated GPU utilization from non-dedicated workloads.

Initial hypothesis:
- Noisy-neighbor / capacity...

title: Dedicated: burst capacity not activating during traffic spikes (Northstar Payments, us-east)
preview:
Issue summary
- Customer reports Dedicated burst capacity did not activate during predictable traffic spikes. Throughput capped at baseline replicas, causing queueing and elevated p95/p99 latency.
- Customer expectation: burst should add temporary replicas within 2–5 minutes when utilization/queue depth crosses thre...
text:
Issue summary
- Customer reports Dedicated burst capacity did not activate during predictable traffic spikes. Throughput capped at baseline replicas, causing queueing and elevated p95/p99 latency.
- Customer expectation: burst should add temporary replicas within 2–5 minutes when utilization/queue depth crosses threshold.

Impact
- 2026-02-12 17:05–18:20 UTC: sustained elevated latency and intermittent 5xx from upstream timeouts due to request queue buildup.
- Customer-visible symptoms:
 - Latency increase: p95 from ~850ms to ~4.8s (chat), p99 up to ~9–12s.
 - RPS capped at baseline; queue depth increased and stayed elevated.
 - No visible scale-out events in their Console view.

Customer / Tier
- Northstar Payments (Dedicated)

Environment
- prod, us-east
- Dedicated pool: northstar-prod-gpu-pool (A100-80GB)

What we expected
- Burst capacity should temporarily scale above reserved b...
links: Customer-provided timestamps + request ID sample set (stored in Support drive; not for external sharing)
Internal dashboard link: Dedicated pool autoscaler events (use Support-safe quick view when sharing internally)

title: 5xx spike during customer A/B test rollout; request configurable auto-rollback thresholds + visibility into rollback decisions
preview:
Customer (Northstar HR) ran an A/B test (baseline model vs candidate quantized variant) on their primary chat completion route and observed a short but sharp increase in HTTP 5xx responses.

They are requesting:
1) Configurable safety rails for A/B and canary rollouts (auto-pause/rollback based on error-rate and lat...
text:
Customer (Northstar HR) ran an A/B test (baseline model vs candidate quantized variant) on their primary chat completion route and observed a short but sharp increase in HTTP 5xx responses.

They are requesting:
1) Configurable safety rails for A/B and canary rollouts (auto-pause/rollback based on error-rate and latency thresholds).
2) Better visibility into rollout decisions: what triggered a pause/rollback, which monitors were evaluated, what thresholds/windows were applied, and links to the supporting metrics.

Issue Summary
- During an A/B test (approx. 10% candidate traffic), 5xx rate spiked above their internal SLO. Customer manually reverted traffic split to baseline.
- Customer states that existing alerts were not actionable enough during the event because they lacked a clear explanation of which part of the rollout was responsible and what the platform would have done automat...

title: GPU OOM during high-churn chat sessions (suspected KV allocator fragmentation) - Northwind Analytics
preview:
Issue summary:
Intermittent GPU OOMs in Northwind Analytics dedicated pool during high-churn chat traffic (many short-lived sessions + cancellations). OOMs occur despite dashboards showing non-zero free GPU memory prior to failure. Initial hypothesis: KV cache arena fragmentation causing allocation failures / forced...
text:
Issue summary:
Intermittent GPU OOMs in Northwind Analytics dedicated pool during high-churn chat traffic (many short-lived sessions + cancellations). OOMs occur despite dashboards showing non-zero free GPU memory prior to failure. Initial hypothesis: KV cache arena fragmentation causing allocation failures / forced request shedding.

Impact:
- Customer saw ~0.6% of chat requests fail with 5xx between 2026-01-26 21:10 UTC and 2026-01-27 02:40 UTC.
- Two pods restarted due to CUDA OOM; transient increased p99 latency (~+1.2s) while pool rebalanced.
- Customer escalated due to concern about stability during upcoming load test.

Customer-reported behavior:
- Failures clustered during bursty load (spikes to 3–4x baseline QPS) with frequent client cancellations (mobile app backgrounding).
- Largest prompts were not the trigger; failures correlated with churn.

Environment:
- Dedicated: nw-...

title: Latency regression after runtime-1.22 canary: elevated p99 decode latency correlated with KV compaction activity
preview:
Issue Summary
- During a runtime-1.22 canary expansion in prod (hosted, us-east-1), we observed a statistically significant p99 latency regression on decode-heavy traffic, most visible for mixed sequence-length distributions.
- Regression correlated with elevated KV compaction work (bytes moved + compaction time) an...
text:
Issue Summary
- During a runtime-1.22 canary expansion in prod (hosted, us-east-1), we observed a statistically significant p99 latency regression on decode-heavy traffic, most visible for mixed sequence-length distributions.
- Regression correlated with elevated KV compaction work (bytes moved + compaction time) and increased allocator fragmentation ratio oscillation.
- Canary was rolled back (feature flag disabled + cohort drained) per guardrails.

Customer Impact
- Customer: Northwind Analytics (enterprise) reported increased p99 latency and intermittent request timeouts during bursty chat traffic.
- Symptoms: p95 largely stable; p99 degraded (notable tail spikes) during bursts and after churn events (rapid session start/stop).
- Scope: Only canary cohort in us-east-1. Control region (eu-west-1) did not show comparable change.

Detection
- 2026-01-29 09:12 PT: SRE alert fired: host...

title: Proxima Bank: unexpected spike in 429s after enterprise priority routing rollout (chat + embeddings)
preview:

Customer (Proxima Bank) reports a sharp increase in HTTP 429 responses starting ~2026-01-20 09:10 PT on /v1/chat/completions and /v1/embeddings. They believe this correlates with Redwood’s recent “priority routing / capacity protection” changes.

text:
## Issue summary
Customer (Proxima Bank) reports a sharp increase in HTTP 429 responses starting ~2026-01-20 09:10 PT on /v1/chat/completions and /v1/embeddings. They believe this correlates with Redwood’s recent “priority routing / capacity protection” changes.

## Impact
- Customer batch + online traffic intermittently fails with 429s, causing retry storms on their side.
- Reported elevated end-user latency due to retries; some requests fail after their max-retry budget.
- Customer concern: enterprise tier expected fewer 429s, not more.

## Environment
- Hosted API, prod
- Region: us-east
- Routes: /v1/chat/completions, /v1/embeddings
- Auth: enterprise API key (account: PROXIMA-ENT-014)

## What customer is seeing
- 429 response body includes Redwood reason codes (examples pasted by customer):
 - "reason": "overload_protection_admission"
 - "reason": "quota_exceeded" (less frequent...

preview:

text:
Issue summary
- Customer (Tensorlytics) reports a sudden ~20–30% drop in throughput (tokens/sec) for their Dedicated pool in eu-west.
- Impact appears isolated to eu-west; us-east Dedicated pool for the same workload unchanged.
- Redwood perf-canary also flagged a tokens/sec regression in eu-west for at least one overlapping model.

Customer impact
- Customer-facing symptoms: longer time-to-first-token (mild) and materially slower generation throughput (primary complaint).
- Customer observed: same prompt + parameters, same concurrency, but higher wall-clock completion times starting ~2026-01-18 08:10 UTC.
- Business impact: degraded SLOs for their production workload; they asked whether a rollback is possible.

Environment
- Deployment mode: Dedicated
- Region: eu-west
- Hardware: H100 pool (80GB) (per fleet inventory)
- Models: llama-3.1-70b-instruct (primary), mixtral-8x7b-instruct...

- Business impact: degraded user experience in an internal “risk analyst copilot” workflow; they paused a batch job that was driving concurrency.

Environment
- Hosted API, prod
- Region: us-east

- Model: rw-chat-3.1-70b (customer-pinned)

title: Dedicated: p95 latency spikes under mixed short/long prompts (head-of-line blocking suspected)
preview:
Issue Summary
Northpeak AI reports intermittent but severe p95 latency spikes on their Dedicated pool when traffic mixes short chat prompts with occasional long-context prompts. Symptoms line up with head-of-line blocking during prefill and decode batch selection.

Customer Impact
- User-visible slow responses (p95)...
text:
Issue Summary
Northpeak AI reports intermittent but severe p95 latency spikes on their Dedicated pool when traffic mixes short chat prompts with occasional long-context prompts. Symptoms line up with head-of-line blocking during prefill and decode batch selection.

Customer Impact
- User-visible slow responses (p95) during business hours.
- Primary pain: chat endpoints where short prompts should remain snappy even when long-context requests are present.
- Observed impact window: 2025-11-24 onward; most noticeable 16:00–22:00 UTC.

Environment
- Deployment: Dedicated
- Region: us-east
- Pool: ded-northpeak-ai-us-east-01
- Runtime version at report: runtime-1.17.9 (baseline scheduler)
- Models: redwood-llama3.1-70b-instruct (primary), redwood-qwen2.5-72b-instruct (secondary)
- Client pattern: mixed prompt-length distribution (short ~0.2–1.5k tokens, long ~10–32k tokens) with bursts of l...

title: Hosted Chat: TTFT + p95 latency regression when long-context traffic increases (mixed prompt lengths)
preview:
Issue summary
Customer reports a regression in Hosted Chat streaming latency (TTFT and p95) when their traffic mix shifts toward long-context prompts. Symptoms match head-of-line blocking in mixed prompt-length workloads (long prefill dominating batch selection), causing short chat requests to wait behind long-prefi...
text:
Issue summary
Customer reports a regression in Hosted Chat streaming latency (TTFT and p95) when their traffic mix shifts toward long-context prompts. Symptoms match head-of-line blocking in mixed prompt-length workloads (long prefill dominating batch selection), causing short chat requests to wait behind long-prefill work.

Impact
- User-visible TTFT regression for short prompts during periods of long-context load.
- Increased p95 for end-to-end latency on streaming chat.
- Customer has a latency SLO for interactive chat; current behavior is impacting production rollouts.

Customer context
- Company: Northpeak AI (enterprise)
- Workload: chat assistant with periodic long-context retrieval (8k–32k context) mixed with short conversational turns.
- Client uses streaming responses and measures TTFT as time until first token chunk.

Environment
- Hosted API, prod, region: us-east
- Models...

text:
Issue summary
- Customer reported intermittent 5xx responses from Redwood Private inference endpoints. Spikes correlate with KMS throttling errors during envelope decrypt.

Impact
- Symptoms: elevated 5xx at API gateway, increased p95 latency, sporadic request failures (non-deterministic).
- Customer impact: end-user facing errors during peak traffic windows; retries from upstream increased load and made spikes worse.
- Duration: recurring spikes over ~3 days; most acute on 2025-11-19 15:10–16:05 ET.

Environment
- Deployment: Redwood Private
- Region: us-east-1
- Cloud/KMS: AWS KMS (customer-managed key)
- Workloads: chat + embeddings, moderate concurrency with periodic burst traffic

Detection / signals
- Alert: "KMS dependency error rate" (new dashboard pack) triggered at 15:14 ET.
- Observed metrics:
 - redwood.kms.requests_total increased sharply with retries

title: Quarantine policy during Dedicated expansion quarantined too many nodes, causing temporary capacity shortfall (us-east A100 pool)
preview:

text:
Issue summary
- Customer (Northwind Analytics) reported a throughput drop after initiating a Dedicated pool expansion in us-east (A100). Expansion nodes were provisioned but a high fraction were automatically moved to Quarantined during post-provision validation, leading to a temporary capacity shortfall.

Customer impact
- Increased queueing and elevated tail latencies for their Dedicated pool.
- Observed intermittent request timeouts when concurrency exceeded the reduced active capacity.
- No data loss. Customer has fallback disabled and pins model alias; they could not route to hosted/shared capacity.

When
- First customer report: 2025-10-21 09:12 PT
- Peak impact window: ~09:00–11:30 PT

Environment / scope
- Prod, us-east
- Dedicated pool: nw-analytics-ded-us-east-a100-01 (internal pool id: dp_7f3d9f)
- GPU type: A100 80GB
- Expansion request: +24 nodes

title: Private install: CPU/memory saturation alerts became noisy after upgrade (thresholds too tight for inference pods)
preview:
Issue summary:
After upgrading HelioBank’s Redwood Private cluster, Prometheus began firing frequent CPU/memory-related alerts for the serving runtime even though application-level latency/error SLOs remained healthy. Customer reports alert fatigue and requests guidance on tuning + confirmation of whether this is a ...
text:
Issue summary:
After upgrading HelioBank’s Redwood Private cluster, Prometheus began firing frequent CPU/memory-related alerts for the serving runtime even though application-level latency/error SLOs remained healthy. Customer reports alert fatigue and requests guidance on tuning + confirmation of whether this is a known issue with the minimum observability pack.

Impact:
- High-volume alert noise (non-actionable) causing on-call fatigue.
- No customer-facing outage reported; inference remained available.

Customer context:
- Deployment: Redwood Private (on-prem, air-gapped)
- Upgrade: Redwood Private 1.9.4 -> 1.10.0 (same hardware footprint)
- Observability: bundled Prometheus + Grafana; alerts routed to OpsGenie

What’s firing (examples provided by customer):
- RedwoodServingHighCPUUtilization (warning) flapping during normal batch spikes
- RedwoodServingMemoryWorkingSetHigh (warnin...

title: eu-west: burst admission controller timeouts causing elevated 5xx for Dedicated burst requests

text:
Issue summary:
Northpeak Search reports intermittent request failures when attempting to use Dedicated Burst in eu-west. Their baseline Dedicated capacity remains healthy, but when traffic exceeds baseline and routing attempts to borrow burst capacity, requests fail with 5xx.

Impact:
- Affected: burst-eligible traffic (requests that require capacity above reserved baseline).
- Customer-visible symptoms: elevated 503/504 with message "burst admission timeout"; occasional retries succeed.
- Not affected: baseline (non-burst) requests; other regions.

Customer notes:
- Spike window: 2025-05-22 ~10:35–11:20 UTC.
- They observed 3–6% failure rate on burst workload during indexing job.
- They can reproduce by running their weekly indexing job (high sustained QPS + long contexts).

Environment:
- Deployment: Dedicated
- Region: eu-west
- Burst: enabled (beta)
- Gateway: rw-gw-eu-west-3

Ini...

text:
Request: enable the Dedicated staged rollouts beta feature flag for specific Dedicated accounts/pools so CS/TAM + SRE can run staged upgrades with gate evaluation and rollback semantics.

Context:
- Project: "Dedicated staged rollouts for reserved pools"
- We are moving from internal dogfood to limited beta (1–2 design partners) and need scoped enablement by account + pool (+ region where applicable).
- This is NOT general customer self-serve; only for beta accounts with operator involvement.

title: Route experiments: missing experiment_id/variant_id attribution on streaming requests (worse on retries) causing gaps in Console results

text:
Issue Summary
- Customer (Acme AI, route experiments beta) reports that experiment results in Console are inconsistent when using streaming responses. Specifically, p95/p99 latency and cost/token charts show a large percentage of traffic under “Unknown / unattributed” despite the route being under an active experiment.
- Customer verified they are receiving variant headers intermittently on streaming responses.

Customer Impact
- Console experiment dashboards appear incomplete/incorrect (variant comparison not trustworthy).
- Guardrail evaluation confidence reduced because unattributed traffic is excluded from per-variant aggregations.
- Customer is holding ramp from 10% -> 50% until attribution is reliable.

Environment
- Redwood Hosted API via Dedicated capacity.
- Regions: primarily us-east; customer also observed similar behavior after failover to eu-west.
- Endpoints: /v1/chat/co...
